case_title: BAY-LAGUNA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. MARILYN OREM ET AL

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 100094795) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BAY-LAGUNA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION
NOTICE OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT PROVE-UP HEARING (TRANSACTION ID # 100102818) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BAY-LAGUNA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUL-22-2020 CONTINUED TO AUG-26-2020 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
NOTICE OF INTENT TO APPEAR BY TELEPHONE (TRANSACTION ID # 100108023) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BAY-LAGUNA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF BAY-LAGUNA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT OREM, MARILYN AN INDIVIDUAL $10,416.00 ATTORNEY FEES, $5,250.00 FINES AND PENALTIES TO ENFORCE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $15,666.00, SEE SCANNED DOC - ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANT MARILYN OREM, AN INDIVIDUAL, IS PERMANENTLY ENJOINED FROM ENGAGING IN ANY OF THE FOLLOWING BEHAVIORS WITHIN BAY-LAGUNA, INCLUDING THE ASSOCIATION'S COMMON AREAS: (1) PREVENTING RESIDENTS FROM LEAVING THE ELEVATOR; (2) LEAVING THREATENING NOTES ON VEHICLES OF RESIDENTS; (3) VERBALLY ATTACKING RESIDENTS FOR USING THE STAIRS AND LAUNDRY FACILITY; (4) OPENING PACKAGES IN THE LOBBY AND TAKING THE CONTENTS; (5) SENDING ANY EMAIL OR MAKING ANYTELEPHONE CALL TO THE ASSOCIATION'S PROPERTY MANAGER; (6) MAKING ANTI-SEMITIC SLURS AND OTHER OFFENSIVE COMMENTS TO RESIDENTS; AND (7) ENGAGING IN ANY SIMILAR BEHAVIOR THAT UNREASONABLY INTERFERES WITH ANY OWNER'S, MEMBER'S OR OTHER RESIDENT'S USE AND ENJOYMENT OF THE ASSOCIATION'S COMMON AREAS.
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, (TRANSACTION ID # 100140019): TARAN, JESSICA SUBSTITUTED FOR TROST, THOMAS G AS ATTORNEY FOR BAY-LAGUNA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Off Calendar

Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-27-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Marilyn Orem, an individual.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Jason Sherman, Esq. appeared via CourtCall for plaintiff Bay-Laguna Condominium Owners' Association, a California nonprofit mutual benefit corporation.
Carol Elbert and Cary Schneider, Community Manager and Board Member of the Association, respectively, were placed under oath and testified.
Both Ms. Elbert and Mr. Schneider appeared via CourtCall for this morning's proceedings.
Defendant Marilyn Orem, an individual, was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Enforcement of Governing Document, Injunctive Relief, Declaratory Relief and Specific Performance was filed on October 24, 2019, and personally served on defendant Marilyn Orem, an individual, on December 4, 2019.
Default as to defendant Marilyn Orem, an individual, was entered on January 7, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on January 24, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Bay-Laguna Condominium Owners' Association, a California nonprofit mutual benefit corporation, and against the defaulted defendant Marilyn Orem, an individual, as follows:

Fines and Penalties - $5,250.00.
Attorney Fees - $10,416.00.

Total - $15,666.00.

Additionally, defendant Marilyn Orem, an individual, is permanently enjoined from engaging in any of the following behaviors within Bay-Laguna, including the Association's common areas:
(1) Preventing residents from leaving the elevator;
(2) Leaving threatening notes on vehicles of residents;
(3) Verbally attacking residents for using the stairs and laundry facility;
(4) Opening packages in the lobby and taking the contents;
(5) Sending any email or making anytelephone call to the Association's property manager;
(6) Making anti-Semitic slurs and other offensive comments to residents; and
(7) Engaging in any similar behavior that unreasonably interferes with any owner's, member's or other resident's use and enjoyment of the Association's common areas.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Continued To Oct-07-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Aug-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marilyn Orem, Defendant in Pro per is present in court.
No other appearances.

Defendant, Marilyn Orem states that she did not view the Courts tentative ruling. Defendant is in non-compliance with the Tentative Ruling rule. The court explains the tentative ruling rule to defendant. Court instructs Defendant that proper legal notice was not provided to plaintiffs and therefore the Court is not allowed to hear legal argument today.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is DENIED. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-02-2020 Continued To Aug-26-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-09-2020 Continued To Aug-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jul-09-2020 Continued To Aug-06-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-11-2020 Continued To Jul-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Marilyn Orem.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Jason Sherman, Esq. appeared via CourtCall for plaintiff Bay-Laguna Condominium Owners' Association.
Also appearing via CourtCall are George Gundry and Cary Schneider, Treasurer and President of the Condominium Association, respectively.
Messsrs. Gundry and Schneider were placed under oath and testified.
Defendant Marilyn Orem was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Enforcement of Governing Document - Injunctive Relief, Declaratory Relief and Specific Performance was filed on October 24, 2019, and personally served on defendant Marilyn Orem on December 4, 2019.
Default as to defendant Marilyn Orem was entered on January 7, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on January 24, 2020.

Good cause appearing, the Court CONTINUES this matter to Thursday, July 9, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the status of defendant's motion to set-aside the default entered January 7, 2020. Ms. Orem is informed that the Court shall move forward with entry of Judgment on July 9th is she (Ms. Orem) has not filed her motion to set-aside the default.
